# Expert orchestration

How a block's feedforward path is realized: MLP, GLU, KAN, PEER, ...

Registry: ``praxis.EXPERT_REGISTRY`` (7 entries)

## `arc` - ArcGLU

GLU with per-depth activation specialization.

Source: [praxis/dense/arc.py:13](../praxis/dense/arc.py#L13)

## `glu` - GatedLinearMLP

A standard MLP, augmented with Gated Linear Units.

Source: [praxis/dense/glu.py:15](../praxis/dense/glu.py#L15)

## `kan` - KolmogorovArnoldNetwork

An implementation of an approximate Kolmogorov-Arnold Network, of which is a theoretical
alternative to the traditional Multi-Layer Perceptron. We use an approximate here,
because it is much more efficient than the standard KAN. Code was borrowed from here:
https://github.com/ZiyaoLi/fast-kan

Source: [praxis/dense/kan.py:16](../praxis/dense/kan.py#L16)

## `mlp` - MultiLayerPerceptron

A standard Multi-Layer Perceptron.

Source: [praxis/dense/mlp.py:14](../praxis/dense/mlp.py#L14)

## `peer` - ParameterEfficientExpertRetrieval

This class implements the Parameter-Efficient Expert Retrieval (PEER) mechanism:
https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.04153v1

Source: [praxis/dense/peer.py:15](../praxis/dense/peer.py#L15)

## `poly` - PolynomialExpansionMLP

A novel dense layer based on explicit polynomial feature expansions. Learns combinations
of features up to a specified degree while maintaining computational efficiency through
careful parameter sharing. Polynomial functions are universal approximators (Stone-
Weierstrass theorem).

Source: [praxis/dense/poly.py:15](../praxis/dense/poly.py#L15)

## `scatter` - ScatterMLP

A scatter-based neural network layer that dynamically modifies weights during the
forward pass based on input features.

Source: [praxis/dense/scatter.py:15](../praxis/dense/scatter.py#L15)
